perturbation.original_text: This agreement does not grant or authorize the Authorized Person to have unescorted direct access to KDOL systems . Additionally, execution of this agreement alone does not fulfill all requirements to permit Authorized Person access to confidential informa tion. Additional requirements must also be satisfied, including but not limited to the requirements found in Section 5.11 of the SSA TSSR v.7; requirements of the Information Exchange Agreement between the Social Security Administration and the State of K ansas Department of Labor; and the terms and conditions set forth in the Computer Matching and Privacy Protection Act Agreement (“CMPPA Agreement”) between SSA and the State of Kansas, governing the State Agency’s use of the data disclosed from SSA’s Priva cy Act System of Records .
perturbation.changed_text: This agreement grants and authorizes the Authorized Person to have unescorted direct access to KDOL systems. Additionally, execution of this agreement alone fulfills all requirements to permit Authorized Person access to confidential information. No Additional requirements must also be satisfied.
perturbation.explanation: This change contradicts the original condition that the agreement alone isn't sufficient for access and that additional security requirements must be met. This change could contradict the section relating to the requirement to comply with all data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075 for a ll information systems. The original text explicitly states that further requirements exist, while the changed text completely negates this. This creates uncertainty as to whether Authorized Person needs to meet additional security protocols or not.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Authorized Person agrees to comply with all data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075 for a ll information systems used for receiving, processing, storing, or transmitting confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement. Both parties shall exercise reas onable and prudent security procedures to protect such information, reports, returns and other documentation in their possession, including electronic versions thereof, from any unauthorized access and/or disclosure. Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be l imited to the minimum number of individuals necessary to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement alone fulfills all requirements for access to confidential information and that no additional requirements must be satisfied. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the Authorized Person agrees to comply with data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075 for all information systems used for receiving, processing, storing, or transmitting confidential information obtained pursuant to the agreement. The changed text eliminates the need for additional requirements, while the contradicted text explicitly states that data storage requirements must be met, creating a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees that they will not provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, unless authorized in writing by KDOL, and then conditioned upon said agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Person agrees that they are encouraged to provide or allow access of the information obtained hereunder to any agent, third party, contractor, subcontractor, or any other public agency, without needing authorization in writing by KDOL, or that the agent, third party, contractor, s ubcontractor, or any other public agency agreeing in writing to be bound by the use and confidentiality terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This change suggests Authorized Person is encouraged to provide access to third parties, removing the requirement for KDOL's written authorization and agreement to confidentiality terms. This contradicts the entire purpose of the confidentiality agreement, as it removes the protection from unauthorized disclosure. This is an in-text contradiction because the core purpose of the document, established at the beginning, is to ensure confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Access to any confidential information is specifica lly conditioned upon compliance with the following: Use of Confidential Information The term “confidential i nformation ” shall include : any information or data maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, and includes personally identifiable information (PII) maintained by KDOL such as: • The name of a person • His or her mailing address • His or her taxpayer identification number • Email addresses • Telephone numbers • Social Security Numbers • Bank account numbers • Date and place of birth • Mother’s maiden name • Biometric data (e.g., height, weight, eye color, fingerprints) • The name or identity of an employing unit • Records described in K.A.R. 50- 2-2 • Any combination of the preceding
perturbation.justification: The original text required written authorization from KDOL before allowing third-party access to confidential information, and mandated that such third parties agree in writing to confidentiality terms. The changed text encourages providing access to third parties without KDOL authorization or agreement to confidentiality terms. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which states that access to confidential information is 'specifically conditioned upon compliance' with confidentiality requirements and defines confidential information. The changed text removes the condition of compliance, thus creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Authorized Person agrees that any confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information shall be in aggregate form, and shall not in any way contain personally identifiable information or information which alone, or in combination with other information, is linked, or is linkable, to a specific individual, and which would thereby allow a reasonable perso n in the community, who does not have personal knowledge of the relevant circumstances, to identify the individual with reasonable certainty.
perturbation.changed_text: Authorized Person agrees that any confidential information or reports generated from such confidential information may contain personally identifiable information or information which alone, or in combination with other information, is linked, or is linkable, to a specific individual, and which would thereby allow a reasonable perso n in the community, who does not have personal knowledge of the relevant circumstances, to identify the individual with reasonable certainty.
perturbation.explanation: This change allows the inclusion of PII in confidential information or reports, directly contradicting the previous requirement that such information be in aggregate form only. This creates ambiguity and conflicts with the initial definition of confidential information, as well as the purpose of protecting individual privacy.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The term “confidential i nformation ” shall include : any information or data maintained by KDOL that has been obtained pursuant to the administration of the Employment Security Law, and includes personally identifiable information (PII) maintained by KDOL such as: • The name of a person • His or her mailing address • His or her taxpayer identification number • Email addresses • Telephone numbers • Social Security Numbers • Bank account numbers • Date and place of birth • Mother’s maiden name • Biometric data (e.g., height, weight, eye color, fingerprints) • The name or identity of an employing unit • Records described in K.A.R. 50- 2-2 • Any combination of the preceding
perturbation.justification: The original text strictly prohibited PII in confidential information. The changed text explicitly allows PII, directly contradicting the definition of 'confidential information' in the 'Contradicted Text' which states that the term includes PII. This creates a clear in-text contradiction, as what is defined as confidential information is now directly conflicting with a section prohibiting it. Therefore, the answer is YES.

perturbation.original_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person agrees to immediately destroy all such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media subject to any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board , in the following manner: Confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement, and any paper material generated therefrom, such as copies, photo impressions, computer printouts, notes, and work papers, must be destroyed by burning or shredding.
perturbation.changed_text: Once confidential information or data obtained hereunder is no longer useful for the purposes described herein, Authorized Person may retain such confidential information or data maintained on any information system platform or any form of storage media indefinitely, regardless of any schedule of retention established by the Kansas Records Board .
perturbation.explanation: This change allows for indefinite retention of confidential information, directly contradicting the original requirement for immediate destruction once the information is no longer useful. This creates a contradiction with other provisions regarding data security and disposal, as well as conflict with any external data retention policies. Also contradicts with the section that states 'Authorized Person agrees to comply with all data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075', since data retention is covered there.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Authorized Person agrees to comply with all data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075 for a ll information systems used for receiving, processing, storing, or transmitting confidential information obtained pursuant to this agreement. Both parties shall exercise reas onable and prudent security procedures to protect such information, reports, returns and other documentation in their possession, including electronic versions thereof, from any unauthorized access and/or disclosure. Access to the information covered by this agreement shall be l imited to the minimum number of individuals necessary to achieve the stated purpose of the agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for indefinite retention of confidential information, which directly contradicts the requirement in the contradicted text that 'Authorized Person agrees to comply with all data storage requirements in IRS Publication 1075'. The original text required immediate destruction, reinforcing the compliance requirement, while the changed text removes this and permits indefinite retention, creating a direct conflict with the storage requirements mandated by IRS Publication 1075.
